blurb: The iconic nut pine of the Southwest mesas — a slow, drought-proof, rounded evergreen that, in good years, drops the sweet, rich pine nuts of pesto and tradition.
seasons: Evergreen blue-green paired needles year-round; rounded cones with edible nuts in fall of good years; reddening bark with age
wildlife: Pine nuts feed jays, nutcrackers, squirrels, and bears; dense cover for birds
